blurb: The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) regulates medicines and medical devices in the UK, including software and artificial intelligence as a medical device.

hiring.note: MHRA AI-relevant roles are advertised through parent MHRA/Civil Service Jobs, not an AI-specific board.

publications.title: AI Airlock Sandbox Phase 2 Programme Report
publications.summary: MHRA report on the second phase of its AI Airlock regulatory sandbox for AI as a medical device (ran April 2025 to May 2026): methodology for the sandbox, seven case studies, independent-evaluation lessons, and key technical/regulatory insights and recommendations for changes to the AIaMD regulatory and support framework. Not formal MHRA guidance.

publications.title: Software and artificial intelligence (AI) as a medical device
publications.summary: MHRA guidance and change programme on regulating software and AI as a medical device, covering qualification, classification and pre- and post-market requirements.

news.title: MHRA landmark report reveals public views on AI in healthcare
news.summary: MHRA publishes the results of an extensive public consultation on AI in healthcare, which will inform the recommendations of the National Commission into the Regulation of AI in Healthcare and future regulation. Core UK-regulator AI-policy signal: public-attitudes evidence feeding a live UK regulatory-design process. First-party gov.uk first_published_at 2026-06-11T12:04:00+01:00 (content-store verified); sits above the tracked mhra-ai ceiling (AI Airlock Phase 2, 2026-06-09) and alongside the same-day 'Regulation of AI in Healthcare' call-for-evidence outcome.

news.title: UK MHRA leads safe use of AI in healthcare as first country in new global network
news.summary: MHRA becomes the first 'pioneer' country in the HealthAI Global Regulatory Network and references its AI Airlock regulatory sandbox for AI medical devices.

notes: Gov.uk/MHRA collection. Guidance date 2025-02-03 from web_search published metadata for the gov.uk publication page. News date 2025-06-24 confirmed from the article body ('today (24 June)'). Collection content API path returned 404, so items sourced via search + first-party page fetch.
